name: Third-Party Supplier Code of Conduct.pdf
text:
[Page 1]
Meridian Holdings  Frontend Demo Grounded Corpus
POL-034  Supplier Code of Conduct
Controlled synthetic enterprise document — for retrieval, review, and workflow testing.
Page 1
DOCUMENT CONTROL
Third-Party Supplier Code of Conduct
Meridian’s minimum control expectations for strategic vendors and subprocessors.
Document ID
POL-034
Owner
VP, Procurement
Version
1.1
Effective Date
2024-01-09
Audience
Critical suppliers and subprocessors
Synthetic corporate document for demo, evaluation, and retrieval workflows only. All parties, people, systems, repositories, and events are
fictional.
Supplier Obligations
●
SUP-SEC-01. Maintain least-privilege access, MFA for privileged users, and documented approvals for production
support access.
●
SUP-IR-02. Notify Meridian of any material incident involving Meridian data or Meridian-operated workflows within 24
hours of confirmation.
●
SUP-DPA-03. Provide current subprocessor lists and advance notice of material changes.
●
SUP-EVD-04. Retain and furnish reasonable evidence needed for annual review, audit follow-up, and remediation
verification.
●
SUP-BCP-05. Maintain business continuity, backup, and recoverability processes proportionate to the service criticality.
Alignment Purpose
This code is referenced by Procurement, Security, and Legal when reviewing vendor contracts and when converting
contract findings into operational follow-up actions.
